info.name: Football Data API by OddAlerts.com
info.description:
Introduction

The OddAlerts API is geared towards betting and ease of access, allowing you to get your hands dirty with footballing data. The API is structured in a way that's easy to understand. I've been building things with football data for the last 5 years. Now I have a chance to build my own source, with a strong focus on helping those that want to use the data for betting purposes. The 'OA-API' is always improving and adding new endpoints, so stay tuned to Discord, Twitter, and the documentation for updates.

Access

Each request will require an api_key. This can be found on the API page @ OddAlerts.

Pagination

To keep things quick for both OA and the end-user, OddAlerts uses pagination on endpoint where there is potential for a lot of data to be returned. Each endpoint will contain two items: info and data. It's inside the info object that you will find details on the number of total results from your request and the total number of pages. These values make it easy to program your apps to navigate to the next page to fetch all the data.

item.description:
This endpoint allows you to directly access the Fixture Simulation Tool on OddAlerts. You can view an example of the front-end here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GhEApKimr6E

This include also allows for chaining multiple IDs for multiple simulations at once, with a current cap of 10 games per request.

request.description:
Accessing Fixtures

This endpoint allows you to fetch fixtures directly from the OddAlerts DB. Right now, the focus is on those building their own DB for value betting, but the details contained within the fixture will become richer.

The endpoint takes an ID after the /fixtures/, like so:

  • /fixtures/24288763

This will fetch a single record. If you need to grab more than one fixture, you can do so like this:

  • /fixtures/multiple

And you will then add your IDs split only by commas. Here is an example:

  • /fixtures/multiple?ids=24288763,24288764

Including Additional Data

With any of the fixture endpoints, you can include the following data:

  • Probability (Markets)

  • Stats (In-Game / In-Play)

  • CorrectScores (Probability)

  • Odds (Pre-Match)

  • Live Odds (Bet365)

  • H2H History

You can include these like so:

  • &include=stats,probability,odds,correctScores,odds.live,h2h

Filtering Odds Types & Bookmakers:

By default, including odds on fixtures will show the closing (latest) odds in the DB from our collection of bookmakers. You can also specify to see the opening odds, or peak odds like so:

  • odds_type=peak

  • odds_type=opening

  • odds_type=closing

You can also select your bookmaker using the following IDs when filtering by bookmaker is supported:

  1. Pinnacle

  2. Bet365

  3. 1xBet

  4. WilliamHill

  5. Betfair Exchange

  6. Kambi Group

Filtering for Competitions, Seasons, and Teams

You can filter on any of the /fixture endpoints by using these parameters:

  • teams=xxx

  • seasons=xxx

  • competitions=xxx

You can filter for a maximum of 25 items per filter. So for example, you could do:

fixtures/upcoming?seasons=6484,6377,6376,6380,6411

and to view upcoming games from just the latest EFL seasons. This will allow you to also find games for certain teams, like so:

fixtures/upcoming?teams=10719

This will give you the upcoming games (next 14 days) for Fulham. Again, you can include 25 teams in this filter.

The games are ranked not just by the # of times they have been viewed, but the # of times they have been found and discovered on the site with filters, value bets, and other tools.

request.description: This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve live fixtures data from the OddAlerts API. The request includes the API token as a query parameter and also specifies the inclusion of statistics, probability, and odds related to the fixtures. The live odds can be included with odds.live and will show odds that were found and saved within the last 90 seconds.

response.name: Live Fixtures

item.name: Odds

request.description:
This request allows you to view the odds history (including the latest odds for upcoming games) for hundreds of thousands of fixtures. To access this data, you just have to include the ID of the game, like so:

/odds/50299715

If you would like to fetch multiple games (with a limit of 50 at this moment in time) then you can do so like this:

/odds/multiple&ids=50299715,50299716

You can filter the results to include only the markets you are looking for by adding the following parameters:

&markets=4&bookmakers=1

This example will grab the FT Result odds from Pinnacle.

response.name: History
response.body:
{

request.description:
This request allows you to access the odds movement in the buildup and shortly after a fixture on OddAlerts. Just like the odds history endpoint, you can filter by bookmaker, market, or outcome. For example, if you wanted to get the home win odds movement, you could take a look at the following:

&markets=6&outcomes=home

These parameters allow multiple entries so you can look at the half-time result and the full-time result, or double chance and over 2.5 goals, and the same goes for the outcomes as well. You can mix and match multiple markets and outcomes to get just the odds and markets and lines that you want.

request.description: This endpoint will show you all of the current markets that are used for bet tracking and odds movement on OddAlerts. These IDs can be used across different endpoints for filtering, as well as upcoming additions to the API (such as saving your own bets to Bet Tracking).

response.name: Markets

request.description:
An incredibly powerful endpoint, this allows you to view the latest dropping odds in the OddAlerts DB. It will show you the top 5000 records for dropping odds, also allowing for customisation of the markets, bookmakers, fixtures, seasons, and competitions.

For example, you could do:

- odds/dropping?seasons=6484,6377,6376,6380,6411

And you'll see the dropping odds (if any) for the EFL seasons for 24/25. You can include up to 25 IDs for fixtures, competitions, and seasons.

response.name: Dropping

request.description:
Latest Odds Movement Records

Live feed of the most recent odds movements across all upcoming fixtures, ordered newest-first. Filterable and paginated.

Query parameters

Param Type Default Description
bookmakers csv ints all Front-facing bookmaker IDs (see /bookmakers). e.g. 1,2,3
markets csv ints all Market IDs from /odds/markets. e.g. 1,28091
outcomes csv strings all Bet names. e.g. home,draw,away or over_25,under_25
fixtures csv ints all Restrict to specific fixture IDs. Max 100.
since_minutes int 5 Look-back window in minutes. Range 1–1440 (24h).
page int 1 Page number.
per_page int 250 Results per page. Max 500.

Common Usages:

Live Ticker:

GET /api/odds/latest?since_minutes=1

Pinnacle & Bet365 Movements (FT Result, Last Hour):

GET /api/odds/latest?bookmakers=1,2&markets=1&since_minutes=60

Under 2.5 Movements (Last Hour):

GET /api/odds/latest?outcomes=over_25,under_25&since_minutes=10

Live Feed for Specific Fixtures:

GET /api/odds/latest?fixtures=24288763,29120448&since_minutes=60

item.description: With the OddAlerts API, you can access the Odds History. At the time of writing, this includes ~8.2m records from the last 6 months, including the opening, closing, and peak odds for a wide-range of markets. We plan to add historical odds for games beyond 6 months after the launch of the API. You can expect this towards the fall of 2023.

request.description: This endpoint allows you to see all of your bankrolls from OddAlerts.com - You can create bankrolls and add bets to your bankrolls using the front-end on the site. You can then use the API to monitor your bets as they settle, calculate profits, and create further systems.

response.name: Bankrolls

request.description:
This endpoint allows you to view all of your latest bets within Bet Tracking on OddAlerts. You can filter by bankroll by adding the following parameter to the URL:

  • &bankrolls=YOUR_ID

You can chain the IDs if you want to load multple, like:

  • &bankrolls=ID,ID,ID

You can sort the feed by the following options:

  • created_at

  • unix (fixture kick off)

  • odds

response.name: Bets

request.description:
This HTTP GET request retrieves statistics for a specific season using the season ID and API token. The request includes a query parameter 'include_frozen' which is set to false. You can set this to true to include all of the frozen stats. They will have the fixture_id set and this is the stats as they were before kick-off in that game. The response will include information about the teams' performance in the specified season, such as matches played, won, drawn, lost, points, goals, fouls, and more.

Available Settings and Includes

Last X Games

This allows you to view the recent form of the teams within a season. For example, adding &last_x=10_overall will bring you the last 10 games worth of data. You can comibine any number with home, away, overall to find the form you're looking for.

Include AVG

When adding &include_avg=true to the call, you'll see a team that represents the average of the league and settings you're looking at. Very useful when comparing how a team is doing vs the rest of the teams in the league.

response.name: Team Stats by Season
response.body:

request.description:
This HTTP GET request retrieves statistics for a specific season using the season ID and API token. The request includes a query parameter 'include_frozen' which is set to false. You can set this to true to include all of the frozen stats. They will have the fixture_id set and this is the stats as they were before kick-off in that game. The response will include information about the teams' performance in the specified season, such as matches played, won, drawn, lost, points, goals, fouls, and more.

Available Settings and Includes

Last X Games

This allows you to view the recent form of the teams within a season. For example, adding &last_x=10_overall will bring you the last 10 games worth of data. You can comibine any number with home, away, overall to find the form you're looking for.

Include AVG

When adding &include_avg=true to the call, you'll see a team that represents the average of the league and settings you're looking at. Very useful when comparing how a team is doing vs the rest of the teams in the league.

request.description:
This request shows you how you can fetch the latest X games for all teams from a season, a really powerful include. For example:

last_x=10_overall

Will pull in the last 10 games for all teams and present the stats across all competitions. If you want the last X form to be for the competition you're looking at, then you can set all_comps to false and it'll work with the current season only.

Here's the available options:

Form Number Location
1-25 home,away,overall

For example, you might do:

  • 5_away

  • 15_home

  • 20_overall

or any other combination.

response.name: Team Stats by Season

request.description:
This endpoint allows you to explore the probability model. It ranks upcoming (or historic) games by probability highest to lowest. You can view games by entering the ID or SLUG of the market you desire.

By default, it will show you all outcomes within a certain market. For example, requesting ft_result will show:

  • home

  • draw

  • away

But if you just wanted to see the top games for home win probability, you could add:

  • &outcome=home

response.name: Probability Rankings

request.description: This endpoint allows you to explore the probability model markets.

response.name: Probability Markets

request.description:
This endpoint gives you access to the powerful value bets that can be found on OddAlerts. It gives you access to the raw data, including the odds from multiple bookmakers.

This endpoint has two TYPES:

  • api/value/upcoming

  • api/value/results

The first will show you all upcoming value bets on OddAlerts, whilst the latter will show you the games that have recently finished. This setting is used for updating your database, app, or spreadsheet.

You can also filter for a specific market by including the market parameter, like so:

/value/upcoming&market=homeWin

Here is the full list of available market filters:

  • homeWin

  • draw

  • awayWin

  • btts

  • o15

  • u15

  • o25

  • u25

  • o35

  • u35

  • homeGoals

  • awayGoals

  • o85Corners

And you can filter on the bookmakers, like so:

  • &bookmakers=6,7

  • &bookmakers=6

If you want to view multiple markets, you can chain them like so: homeWin,btts,draw

You can use the &duration parameter to adjust the time frame that games are found from. For example, adding:

&duration=43200

to your endpoint will show you value bets for the next 12 hours. This value is in seconds. If you're using the /results endpoint, it will show you results from the last 12 hours.

You can also sort the value bets you see by like so:

&sort=time

By default, value bets are sorted by time, but you can sort by any of the following data points:

  • odds

  • probability

  • value

  • peak_odds

  • our_odds

request.description:
Value Bets — Your Strategies

These endpoints let you view your configured value bet strategies and retrieve upcoming fixtures that match them. You can edit your settings for this endpoint here. All filters from your strategy settings (probability range, odds range, value range, bookmaker, predictability, etc.) are applied automatically.

GET /value/you

Returns a list of your configured value bet strategies. Each strategy has a unique id that you use with the next endpoint.

Authentication: Requires api_token

Example Request:

GET /api/value/you?api_token=YOUR_TOKEN

Example Response:

{ "info": { "count": 16 }, "data": [ { "id": "home_win_probability", "text": "Home Win", "strategy_label": "Kambi Group Closing", "market_key": "ft_result", "market_id": 6, "bet": "home", "base_market": "home_win_probability", "odds_type": "closing", "bookmaker": "6", "range": ["42", "100"], "odds": ["2", "100"], "value_range": ["5", "35"], "min_played": "0", "league_progress": [0, 100], "league_predictability": { "any": false, "poor": true, "medium": true, "good": true, "high": true }, "exclude": { "cups": false, "friendlies": true }, "automation": false, "cover_fixture_lifetime": false } ]}

Value Bets — Your Strategies

These endpoints let you view your configured value bet strategies and retrieve upcoming fixtures that match them. You can edit your settings for this endpoint here. All filters from your strategy settings (probability range, odds range, value range, bookmaker, predictability, etc.) are applied automatically.

GET /value/you/{id}

Returns upcoming fixtures that match a specific strategy's filters. The {id} comes from the /value/you endpoint above.

Probability, odds, and value filters from your strategy are applied automatically. Each returned fixture includes a value field showing the calculated value percentage.

Authentication: Requires api_token

Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description
from Unix timestamp now Start of date range
to Unix timestamp now + 14 days End of date range
include string probability,odds Comma-separated. probability and odds are always included. Additional options: stats, h2h, correctScores, timings, odds.live
competitions string — Filter by competition IDs (comma-separated)
seasons string — Filter by season IDs (comma-separated)
teams string — Filter by team IDs (comma-separated)
per_page integer 250 Results per page (max 250)
page integer 1 Page number

Example Request:

GET /api/value/you/u25_probability?api_token=YOUR_TOKEN

With date range and additional includes:

GET /api/value/you/o25_probability?api_token=YOUR_TOKEN&from=1774600000&to=1775200000&include=stats,h2h

GET /referees

Returns a paginated list of all referees.

Query Parameters

Parameter Type Default Description
search string — Filter referees by name (partial match)
page int 1 Page number
per_page int 50 Results per page (max 250)

GET /referees/upcoming

Returns upcoming fixtures (next 14 days) that have a referee assigned. Uses the same fixture format as /fixtures, supporting all standard includes.

GET /referees/{id}

Returns a single referee with career summary stats and their next upcoming fixture.

GET /referees/{id}/stats

Returns detailed card statistics for a referee, broken down by season.

GET /referees/{id}/seasons

Returns a lightweight list of all seasons a referee has officiated in.

GET /referees/{id}/fixtures

Returns a paginated list of fixtures officiated by this referee. Uses the same fixture format as /fixtures, supporting all standard includes.

item.name: Players (Beta)

request.description:
GET /api/players/search

Find a player by name. Returns up to per_page matches against name, common_name and lastname (case-insensitive). Lightweight by default — add ?include=stats,season_stats if you need numbers.

?q= — required, minimum 2 characters. Anything shorter returns 400.

Use it for autocomplete, building a "search a player" UI, or resolving a name to an internal players.id before hitting /players/{id}.

GET /api/players/search?q=cunha
GET /api/players/search?q=haaland&include=stats,season_stats

GET /api/players/{id}

Full profile for a single player by internal players.id. Lightweight by default. Add ?include=stats for rolling form, ?include=season_stats for every season we've ingested for them.

Returns id, team_id (current club), teams[] (current club + national team), names, position_id, detailed_position_id, shirt_number, updated_at, plus the requested optional blocks. 404 if the id doesn't exist.

GET /api/players/143126
GET /api/players/143126?include=stats,season_stats

GET /api/players/rank

Rank every player in the database by a single stat across a single rolling form bucket. The headline use case: "top 25 players for key_passes_per90 over the last 10 games, minimum 5 appearances".

?stat= — required, must be on the whitelist.

?form= — defaults to last_5.

?teams= — optional, max 50, scopes the ranking to players currently in that pool.

Each player includes a highlight block (form, stat, value, apps) so you can render a leaderboard without having to dig through stats.{form} yourself. The highlight is always emitted even without ?include=stats.

GET /api/players/rank?stat=goals_per90&form=last_10&min_apps=5&per_page=25
GET /api/players/rank?stat=points_per_game&form=all&min_apps=10
GET /api/players/rank?stat=assists&form=last_5&teams=10659,11&include=stats

GET /api/players/fixture/{fixture_id}

Every player whose current club is one of the two sides in this fixture. fixture_id is internal fixtures.id. The response includes a fixture block with id, unix, league_id, home_id, away_id so you don't have to round-trip for context.

Sort, filter and form params behave like every other list endpoint. Lightweight by default.

GET /api/players/fixture/19848273
GET /api/players/fixture/19848273?sort_by=shots_total&form=last_5&per_page=50

GET /api/players/season/{season_id}

Every player whose club has a fixture in this season. season_id is internal leagues.id (a specific season of a competition, the same id used everywhere else in the API). Returns a season block with id and competition_id for context.

Two important behaviours specific to this endpoint:

?sort_by runs against this season's totals (season_stats), not rolling form. So ?sort_by=goals genuinely returns the season's top scorers. min_apps and min_minutes are also applied against the season totals. Players whose totals haven't been ingested yet are filtered out of sorted views.

The response includes a single-key season_stats block keyed by season_id for every player who has totals on file — even when you don't pass ?include=season_stats. Add ?include=season_stats if you want every season for each player.

GET /api/players/season/667780?sort_by=goals
GET /api/players/season/667780?sort_by=points_per_game&min_apps=10&per_page=50

GET /api/players/competition/{competition_id}

Same shape as /players/season, but resolves "the current season of this competition" automatically — you don't need to know the live leagues.id. Useful when your client has a competition picker but doesn't track season rollover.

Returns a competition block with id, name and the resolved season_id. Sort and filter behaviour matches /players/season.

GET /api/players/competition/8?sort_by=assists&min_apps=8
GET /api/players/competition/8?sort_by=win_rate&min_apps=15&per_page=20

GET /api/players/meta

Returns the full whitelist of allowed values for every parameter on the player API — sortable stats, form buckets, include values, plus defaults and limits. Use it to populate a dropdown, build a client-side validator, or just discover what's available without grepping through docs.

item.description:
Player Stats API — Endpoint Reference

All endpoints are under https://data.oddalerts.com/api/ and require Authorization: Bearer like the rest of the API. All identifiers in requests and responses are internal OddAlerts IDs (players.id, teams.id, seasons.id, competitions.id, fixtures.id).

Common conventions

?include=stats,season_stats — by default, list endpoints return identity, position, current club and national team only. Add ?include=stats for the rolling form blob (last_5, last_10, last_15, last_20, all), ?include=season_stats for season totals, or both. Anything outside the whitelist is silently dropped.

?form=last_5|last_10|last_15|last_20|all — which rolling bucket to read for ?sort_by, min_apps and min_minutes. Defaults to last_5.

?sort_by= and ?sort=asc|desc — sort by any whitelisted stat. The whitelist covers apps, minutes, goals, assists, shots, tackles, fouls, cards, passes, key passes, crosses, dribbles, duels, interceptions, blocks, cleansheets, saves, the _avg and _per90 variants, the o05/o15/o25 over-line booleans, plus the influence metrics points, points_per_game, wins, win_rate, draws, losses. Anything outside the whitelist returns 400 with the full list. On /players/season and /players/competition, sort_by runs against that season's totals rather than rolling form, so "top scorers in this season" actually means it.

?min_apps=N (default 3), ?min_minutes=N (default 0) — per-bucket floors. Stops single-game players topping _per90 charts. Pass min_apps=0 to opt out.

?position_ids=24,25,26 — CSV of position ids, max 10. Useful for "best defensive midfielders" type queries.

?per_page=N (max 100, default 25, search default 20) and ?page=N for paginated endpoints.

request.description:
The Trends Endpoint

The Trends endpoint will let you view all upcoming fixtures with odds and key stats, sorting these fixtures by a particular stat. With certain trends, like homeWin, you know exactly how the results will be ordered and by which stat. However, for some trends, you can declare a focus. This allows you to sort by the home team or the away teams stats. If you leave this blank, the endpoint will be sorted by the combined stat.

Available Trends

URL Parameter Stat Focus Mode?
homeWin Home Win % ❌
draw Draw % ✅
awayWin Away Win % ❌
btts BTTS % ✅
over15 1.5+ Goals ✅
over25 2.5+ Goals ✅
under25 2.5- Goals ✅
over35 3.5+ Goals ✅
under35 3.5- Goals ✅
avgGoals AVG Goals ✅
1hGoals 1H Goals % ✅
1hCorners 3.5+ 1H Corners % ✅
over8Corners 8.5+ Corners ✅
homeWinHT Home Win HT % ❌
drawHT Draw at HT % ✅
awayWinHT Away Win HT % ❌
boreDraw 0-0 % ✅
under10Corners 10.5- Corners ✅
homeGoals 1.5+ Home Goals % ❌
awayGoals 1.5+ Away Goals % ❌
lateGoals 70+ Mins Goal % ✅
under9Corners 8.5- Corners ✅
btts1h BTTS 1H % ✅
btts2H BTTS 2H % ✅
mostGoals2h Most Goals 2H % ✅

Settings Per Trend

After loading the endpoint, you will see a list of the available settings for you to play with. No settings are required to start getting results. These will allow you to set filters on the odds, the stats, the sorting of the results, and more. Here are the possible options:

Parameter Expected
minOdds Decimal
maxOdds Decimal
sort odds, stat, or time
duration Duration in Seconds
minStat Number
maxStat Number
minPlayed Number
focus "home" or "away"
page Number

description.content: Minimum Odds (Set 1 for Games w/ Odds Only)

request.description: The /correctScores endpoint will give you the next 72 hours of games that have the probability model enables, with a focus on the output for the correct score. You can access the score lines within the scores object. The value you see is the probability that the game will finish with that score, as per the OddAlerts Probability Model.

This endpoint lets you access the competitions in the OddAlerts DB. You can filter by country_ids e.g (&country_ids=1,3,5) or you can use the following URLs if you want to view a specific competition or search for one using text:

  • /competitions/COMP_ID

  • /competitions/search

You can include the following data points:

  • seasons (included all seasons with season ids)

When using the /search, you must also include:

  • &query=Premier League

The search will cover the country and the competition name, so England Premier League might be a better search than just Premier League, as you will find others from around the world for this query.

The Betslip Generator endpoint is available via POST requests. Below you will find examples, types of data accepted, and benefits of the endpoint.

The endpoint allows you to generate betslips using a number of combinations of probability, odds, items per slip, and value bets. Here's a full list of parameters:

  • markets (array of outcomes for the generator)

  • value_bets_only (boolean)

  • duration (time until kick-off, in seconds)

  • target_odds (decimal, e.g 2.5)

  • items_per_slip (e.g 2)

  • odds_per_item (array, e.g [1.2,1.9]

  • max_betslips (e.g 10, max is 50)

  • competitions (list of competition ids)

If you're working in PHP, here's an example of how you might create your settings before sending them to POST:

The markets that are available to use can be found on the /probability/markets endpoint. Any item on that list with a bet_market_id can be used. For the majority of markets, the outcome you will need to add will be: 'yes', or 'no'.

For 'ft_result' and 'ht_result' the outcome(s) are:

  • home

  • draw

  • away

For 'double_chance', the possible outcomes are:

  • home_draw

  • home_away

  • draw_away

Below is an example of how you might add markets to generate betslips for this particular market and outcome:
This request will show you the IDs of the bookmakers that are supported and flowing through our API. We check and update odds movement and odds history for these bookmakers every second.
